prose: Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene (14 atoms: O, O, N, C×6, H×5) computed with the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF backend, density-fit). Single-point calculation on the user-supplied geometry (nitrobenzene.xyz); gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Cation/anion states used charge ±1, multiplicity 2. Charge partitioning via Mulliken population analysis (PySCF). The headline value reported is the maximum f⁺ (most electrophilic site), found at atom 4 (C, ipso carbon bonded to –NO₂): f⁺ = 0.49641780. The most nucleophilic site (max f⁻) is atom 5 (C, ortho carbon): f⁻ = 0.10729. Dual descriptor is most positive at atom 4 (+0.51071), confirming it as the preferred electrophilic/nucleophilic aromatic substitution site. Integrity status: trustworthy = true. Charge-conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). No warnings beyond routine charge-conservation summaries. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 nitrobenzene.xyz.
